task_completion.description: Create three specific folders for the knowledge base in the email client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email client's folder pane displays three newly created folders named exactly 'KB-Security', 'KB-Linux', and 'KB-Development'.

task_completion.description: Move relevant technical emails from the inbox into the newly created folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email client shows that emails have been moved into the 'KB-Security', 'KB-Linux', and 'KB-Development' folders. This can be verified by seeing a populated list of emails when a folder is selected, or by observing unread/total message counts next to the folder names (aiming for at least 5 per folder).

task_completion.description: Create a text file to serve as the knowledge base index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or text editor shows a file named 'kb_index.txt' located in the '~/Documents' (or '/home/ga/Documents') directory.

task_completion.description: Document the categorization, email counts, and topics in the index file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An open text editor displays the contents of 'kb_index.txt', which must include the three category names ('KB-Security', 'KB-Linux', 'KB-Development'), the count of emails in each category, and a brief list or description of the topics covered.

task_completion.description: Draft an email announcing the completion of the knowledge base extraction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An email composition window is open with 'it-team@company.com' in the 'To' field, 'New Knowledge Base' in the 'Subject' field, and a message body that mentions the completion of the extraction and states the total number of articles archived.

integrity.description: The agent used the email client GUI to manage folders and emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with an actual graphical email client (e.g., Thunderbird, Evolution) rather than using terminal commands (like `mv` or `cp`) to manipulate mail directories directly.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor to create and write the index file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (GUI-based like gedit/mousepad or terminal-based like nano/vim) being used to write the contents of 'kb_index.txt', rather than just echoing text into the file via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The announcement email was drafted using the email client's composition interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: An actual email composition window from the email client is visible, rather than a text file mocking an email or a terminal command attempting to send mail directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create exactly three folders: 'KB-Security', 'KB-Linux', and 'KB-Development'. The task targets moving at least 5 emails into each folder (minimum 3 for partial credit). The index file must be saved at '/home/ga/Documents/kb_index.txt' and contain the categories, email counts, and topics. The announcement email must be addressed to 'it-team@company.com' with the exact subject 'New Knowledge Base'. The emails being categorized are real technical emails from the 2002 Apache SpamAssassin public corpus.
